title: machine hooks: closed enforcement via declarations
manager: A machine can declare `hooks: closed`, forbidding any hook not explicitly declared — so a machine that must not be observed or extended in unexpected ways can enforce that at compile time. This is the counterpart to open hooking: it lets a machine's author lock down the observation surface, which matters for sealed and proven builds and for machines whose exact behavior is part of a contract others rely on.
cs: `hooks: closed` enforced against declarations (fsl#617/#645); rejects undeclared hooks; over the uniform registry (W6.77); interacts with sealed/proven builds.
